<item>
      <title>The City of Victory: How Two Captured Brothers Built an Empire from the Ashes — Vijayanagara, Chapter 1</title>
      <description>In this opening chapter, we trace the cataclysmic events that led to the founding of Vijayanagara around 1336 CE — from the relentless southward advance of the Delhi Sultanate under rulers like Ala-ud-din Khilji and the terrifying Muhammad Taghlaq, through the fall of the Hindu fortress of Kampli-Anegundi, to the rise of two brothers, Harihara and Bukka, who — guided by the sage Vidyaranya — established a new city on the Tungabhadra River that would become the last great Hindu empire of southern India. Based on A Forgotten Empire (Vijayanagara) by Robert Sewell (1900).</description>
      <enclosure url="https://drive.google.com/uc?export=download&id=1qSdfFgcf69oqnJdlVY8fqIVjKAjh3FVW" type="audio/mpeg" length="59270834" />
